fab_section_md:
- He was crowned and anointed by the High Septon in the Starry Sept of Oldtown (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:21)
- He put down the ironborn chaos by descending on the islands in 2 AC riding Balerion (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:37)
- — and slew the ironborn claimant himself (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:39)
- He led the invasion of Dorne through the Prince's Pass with an army thirty thousand strong (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:55)
- — but never brought the Dornish to battle. After his sister Rhaenys died at the Hellholt he prosecuted the Dragon's Wroth, then read a sealed letter from Prince Nymor in open court, his hand dripping blood, burned it, and signed peace (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:147)
- In peace he made reconciliation of the Seven Kingdoms his keystone policy (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:173)
- — kept an endless royal progress (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:193)
- — decreed the King's Peace (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:205)
- — and exempted the Faith from taxation (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:209)

- Visenya was the eldest of the three heads of the dragon and outlived both siblings (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:275)
- She rode Vhagar and wielded Dark Sister, appearing above Sisterton to break the Sistermen (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:25)
- She twice defended Aegon from Dornish cutthroats (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:279)
- — trusted no one but her brother (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:279)
- — and once slashed him across the cheek to prove his guards too slow (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:279)
- She founded the Kingsguard and chose its knights herself (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:289)
- According to rumor, in her later years she delved into the dark arts, mixing poisons and casting malign spells (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:275)
- — and some even suggest she was a kinslayer and kingslayer, though no proof was ever offered (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:275)
identity_line: Visenya Targaryen — Visenya was the eldest of the three heads of the dragon and outlived both siblings.

fab_section_md:
- Rhaenys rode Meraxes and opened the First Dornish War in 4 AC, burning the Planky Town (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:53)
- While Aegon marched, she took Lemonwood, Spottswood, and Stinkwater but found no enemy, reporting that "The Yellow / Toad has melted into the sands" (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:81)
- She was a patron of bards and singers (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:255)
- — took a great interest in the smallfolk and women and children (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:259)
- — and her ruling in a wife-beating case gave the realm the "rule of six" (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:265)
- She died at the Hellholt in 10 AC when Meraxes was killed; how she died is disputed. Some say she lost her seat and fell (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:123)
- — others that she was crushed beneath Meraxes (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:123)
- — and a few accounts claim she died by torment in the dungeons of the Ullers (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:123)
identity_line: Rhaenys Targaryen — Rhaenys rode Meraxes and opened the First Dornish War in 4 AC, burning the Planky Town.

fab_section_md:
- Orys was Aegon's bastard half-brother and first Hand (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:233)
- He led one thousand picked knights up the Boneway (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:55)
- — where his men were butchered and he was taken captive by Wyl of Wyl (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:69)
- Ransomed in 7 AC, he returned to find his sword hand lopped off (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:95)
- — and afterward asked to be relieved as Hand (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:233)
- In his bitter later years he argued that Princess Deria be sent home "less a hand" (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:135)
identity_line: Orys Baratheon — Orys was Aegon's bastard half-brother and first Hand.

fab_section_md:
- Meria was Princess of Dorne, the so-called Yellow Toad (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:49)
- Her forces poisoned wells, burned crops, and never offered battle, and she personally threw the royal castellan from the Spear Tower (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:87)
- When Dornish raiders burned the rainwood, she is reported to have said "Fire for fire" (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:101)
- She died abed in 13 AC of age and ill health, having defeated Aegon and his dragons (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:157)
- Her enemies insisted she died in coitus with a stallion (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:135)
identity_line: Meria Martell — Meria was Princess of Dorne, the so-called Yellow Toad.

fab_section_md:
- Nymor was Meria's son, sixty years old and failing, who succeeded her as Lord of Sunspear and Prince of Dorne (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:135)
- Wanting no more slaughter, he sent an embassy led by his daughter Deria to return the skull of Meraxes and offer peace (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:135)
identity_line: Nymor Martell — Nymor was Meria's son, sixty years old and failing, who succeeded her as Lord of Sunspear and Prince of Dorne.

fab_section_md:
- Wyl of Wyl was a savage mountain lord called the Widow-lover (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:69)
- / "mountain lord called Widow-lover". He captured Orys Baratheon on the Boneway and later maimed his ransomed captives' sword hands. In 12 AC he came uninvited to a wedding, slew Lord Oakheart and the guests, gelded the groom, and had the bride raped and sold (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:131)
- He survived Aegon's dragonfire in caves and lived another twenty years (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:97)
identity_line: Wyl of Wyl was a savage mountain lord called the Widow-lover.

fab_section_md:
- Harlan Tyrell, Warden of the South, boasted his host could smash any Dornish army (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:57)
- Sent against Uthor Uller, he lost a quarter of his men to poisoned wells and the sun before reaching an abandoned Hellholt (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:63)
- Setting off across the red sands, he and his entire army vanished (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:91)
identity_line: Harlan Tyrell, Warden of the South, boasted his host could smash any Dornish army.

fab_section_md:
- Qhorin Volmark of Harlaw, whose grandmother was a younger sister of Harren's grandsire, declared himself rightful heir of the black line (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:31)
- He assumed the ironborn kingship but was slain by Aegon with Blackfyre in 2 AC; his infant son was allowed to inherit his lands (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:39)
identity_line: Qhorin Volmark of Harlaw, whose grandmother was a younger sister of Harren's grandsire, declared himself rightful heir of the black line.

fab_section_md:
- Lodos was a barefoot holy man crowned on Old Wyk who proclaimed himself the living son of the Drowned God (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:35)
- — and was said to be able to work miracles (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:35)
- When the krakens did not rise, he filled his robes with stones and walked into the sea; thousands followed (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:39)
identity_line: Lodos was a barefoot holy man crowned on Old Wyk who proclaimed himself the living son of the Drowned God.

fab_section_md:
- Vickon Greyjoy, Lord Reaper of Pyke, was chosen by the ironborn as their own lord paramount (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:43)
- He did homage to King Aegon and renounced the mainland lands House Hoare had seized (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:45)
identity_line: Vickon Greyjoy, Lord Reaper of Pyke, was chosen by the ironborn as their own lord paramount.

fab_section_md:
- The Three Sisters crowned Lady Marla of House Sunderland their queen during the Conquest's chaos (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:25)
- Her people deposed her in favor of her younger brother when Visenya appeared (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:25)
- Exiled and imprisoned, after five years her tongue was removed and she lived out her days with the silent sisters (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:27)
identity_line: Marla Sunderland — The Three Sisters crowned Lady Marla of House Sunderland their queen during the Conquest's chaos.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Osmund Strong was Aegon's fourth Hand, named after Alton Celtigar's death in 17 AC (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:233)
- He and Grand Maester Gawen were charged with building King's Landing's walls (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:229)
identity_line: Osmund Strong — Ser Osmund Strong was Aegon's fourth Hand, named after Alton Celtigar's death in 17 AC.

fab_section_md:
- Edmyn Tully, Lord of Riverrun, was Aegon's second Hand, serving 7–9 AC (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:233)
- When his wife died in childbed he begged leave to return to the riverlands (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:233)
identity_line: Edmyn Tully, Lord of Riverrun, was Aegon's second Hand, serving 7–9 AC.

fab_section_md:
- Aenys was Aegon and Rhaenys's son and heir, six years old at the time of the Dornish peace (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:155)
- Aegon told him a lord should know the land he rules (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:199)
identity_line: Aenys Targaryen — Aenys was Aegon and Rhaenys's son and heir, six years old at the time of the Dornish peace.

fab_section_md:
- Queen Visenya kept a hirsute hunchback fool called Lord Monkeyface (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:269)
- When he choked to death on a peach pit she dressed an ape in his clothing (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:269)
identity_line: Lord Monkeyface — Queen Visenya kept a hirsute hunchback fool called Lord Monkeyface.

fab_section_md:
- Archmaester Ollidar, keeper of histories, was the first Grand Maester, his ring and rod and mask of bronze (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:245)
- Exceptionally old, he died less than a year into the office (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:245)
identity_line: Ollidar — Archmaester Ollidar, keeper of histories, was the first Grand Maester, his ring and rod and mask of bronze.

fab_section_md:
- Archmaester Lyonce, chosen after Ollidar, had a ring, rod, and mask of yellow gold (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:245)
- He served until 12 AC, when he slipped in the mud, broke his hip, and died (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:245)
identity_line: Lyonce — Archmaester Lyonce, chosen after Ollidar, had a ring, rod, and mask of yellow gold.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Joffrey Dayne led a Dornish army from Starfall against Oldtown (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:111)
- — and slew Lord Hightower's younger son Garmon (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:111)
identity_line: Joffrey Dayne — Ser Joffrey Dayne led a Dornish army from Starfall against Oldtown.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Addam was sent by his father to retake Nightsong but found it torched (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:111)
- On old Lord Manfred's death he succeeded as Lord of the High Tower (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:115)
identity_line: Addam Hightower — Ser Addam was sent by his father to retake Nightsong but found it torched.

fab_section_md: - Alton Celtigar, Lord of Claw Isle, replaced Edmyn Tully as Hand and served until his death from natural causes in 17 AC (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:233)
identity_line: Alton Celtigar, Lord of Claw Isle, replaced Edmyn Tully as Hand and served until his death from natural causes in 17 AC.

fab_section_md: - Lord Jon Rosby was named Castellan of Sunspear and Warden of the Sands to rule Dorne in the king's name (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:83)
identity_line: Jon Rosby — Lord Jon Rosby was named Castellan of Sunspear and Warden of the Sands to rule Dorne in the king's name.

fab_section_md:
- Ser Quenton Qoherys, Aegon's master-at-arms on Dragonstone, was granted Harrenhal (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:47)
- — under Lord Edmyn Tully as his liege (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:47)
- A widower, he agreed to wed one of Tully's daughters (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:49)
identity_line: Quenton Qoherys — Ser Quenton Qoherys, Aegon's master-at-arms on Dragonstone, was granted Harrenhal.

fab_section_md: - Theo Tyrell, the young Lord of Highgarden and Warden of the South, was reluctant to invade Dorne again after his father's fate (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:115)
identity_line: Theo Tyrell, the young Lord of Highgarden and Warden of the South, was reluctant to invade Dorne again after his father's fate.

fab_section_md: - Ser Jon Cafferen, heir to Fawnton, was wed to Alys Oakheart when the Widow-lover's men gelded him at his own wedding (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:131)
identity_line: Jon Cafferen — Ser Jon Cafferen, heir to Fawnton, was wed to Alys Oakheart when the Widow-lover's men gelded him at his own wedding.

fab_section_md: - Alys Oakheart, daughter of the Lord of Old Oak, was forced to watch her husband's gelding, then raped and sold to a Myrish slaver (fab-reign-of-the-dragon-04:131)
identity_line: Alys Oakheart, daughter of the Lord of Old Oak, was forced to watch her husband's gelding, then raped and sold to a Myrish slaver.
